items.title: Free SaaS Landing Page Template – React, Vue, HTML for Claude Code and Cursor
items.why_it_matters: A developer released a free, clean SaaS landing page template optimized specifically for Claude Code and Cursor. Since you are trying to ship AI-ingest as a revenue project, you don't need to waste time designing a frontend from scratch. This week, feed this template's repository to Claude Code and ask it to customize the copy and components for your AI-ingest launch page.

items.title: Show HN: An MCP server that gives your AI assistant write access to /etc./hosts
items.why_it_matters: LockIn is an MCP server that lets your AI assistant block distracting websites (like Twitter or Reddit) by editing your system's hosts file directly. Since your biggest hurdle right now is getting distracted by the AI news cycle instead of shipping, you can use your AI to gatekeep your focus. This week, install this MCP server and tell Claude Code to block Twitter for a three-hour deep-work session while you build.

items.title: Show HN: Crew – Let Claude Code agents talk to each other
items.why_it_matters: Crew is a new open-source tool that lets you run multiple Claude Code sessions concurrently on the same repository without dealing with Git worktree conflicts, allowing the agents to communicate and coordinate. This is perfect for your workflow because you can delegate different tasks to different Claude sessions simultaneously. This week, install Crew and have one Claude Code session build your Next.js frontend while another concurrent session sets up your Supabase database schema.

items.title: Calculator built to teach/experiment with costing LLM chat apps
items.why_it_matters: This is an interactive calculator built specifically to help developers estimate and experiment with the API costs of running LLM-based chat applications. Since you are building AI-ingest to be a revenue-generating project, understanding your unit economics is critical before you launch. This week, plug your average prompt and completion token sizes into this calculator to determine exactly how much you need to charge users to stay profitable.

items.title: Overllm – flags where you're paying an LLM to do a regex's job
items.why_it_matters: Overllm is a lightweight tool that scans your codebase to flag areas where you are paying for expensive LLM API calls to perform tasks that a simple, free regular expression (regex) could handle. Reducing token waste is one of the easiest ways to make your side projects financially viable. This week, run Overllm against your AI-ingest repository to see if you can swap out any costly Claude API calls with basic regex patterns.

items.title: Show HN: Local MCP – Claude/ChatGPT read your iMessage, Teams, files on-device
items.why_it_matters: Local MCP is a native macOS app that connects Claude Code and Cursor to your local files, Notes, iMessage, and calendar via MCP tools, keeping all data entirely on your machine. This makes it incredibly easy to feed your local context directly to your development agent without manual copy-pasting. This week, install Local MCP and ask Claude Code to read your local Apple Notes brainstorm file to automatically generate your next feature roadmap.
